docs.title: Issuebeam
docs.text:
← Main website (issuebeam.github.io) · Documentation

Issuebeam wires AI coding agents to GitHub Issues with a stdlib Python CLI and repository instruction files. Works on Windows, macOS, and Linux with Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and more.

Bilingual documentation

This site is also available in Italiano. Use the language selector in the top navigation bar.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Layer Description CLI scripts/github_issue.py — create, list, comment, close, import issues Agent rules AGENTS.md, Cursor rule, Copilot instructions, CLAUDE.md, … GitHub Official backlog with labels, web templates, Fixes #N in PRs

docs.title: Who this is for
docs.text:
You ship with vibe coding (LLMs, fast iteration) and lose track of bugs and tasks buried in chat. Issuebeam moves operational status to GitHub Issues — not scattered markdown files.

Not Cursor-only. The same CLI runs everywhere; only the instruction file path changes per IDE.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
python -c "from pathlib import Path; Path('tracker/github_repo').write_text('myorg/my-app\n')"
python scripts/github_issue.py labels --apply
python scripts/github_issue.py create "Setup test" --labels task
python scripts/github_issue.py list

Details: Overview · Token · Platforms

docs.title: Adopt into your repo
docs.text:
python scripts/adopt.py --target ../my-repo --repo myorg/my-app

Copies CLI, labels, templates, and agent instruction files for multiple platforms.

docs.title: Documentation map
docs.text: Section Content Getting started Problem, architecture, setup Platforms Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, others CLI reference All commands Agent workflow Natural language phrases Reference FAQ, security, troubleshooting

docs.title: Build docs locally
docs.text:
pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
mkdocs serve

Preview at http://127.0.0.1:8000 — publish with python scripts/publish_docs.py, then git push in sibling repo issuebeam.github.io. No GitHub Actions.

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT — Copyright © 2026 Antonio Trento. See LICENSE.

docs.title: Commits and PRs
docs.text:
When a PR resolves tracked work, reference the issue in the commit message:

fix(auth): handle Safari redirect after login

Fixes #42

GitHub closes the issue automatically on merge (if the repo option is enabled).

Variants:

  • Closes #42 — same auto-close behavior
  • Refs #42 — reference without auto-close

docs.title: Agent workflow
docs.text:
After fixing a bug, the agent should:

  1. Commit with Fixes #N when appropriate
  2. Optionally comment on the issue: python scripts/github_issue.py comment N --body "Fixed in …"
  3. Close manually if needed: python scripts/github_issue.py close N

docs.title: Natural language
docs.text:
With agent instructions active (AGENTS.md, Cursor rule, Copilot, …) describe work in plain language — no need to memorize CLI syntax.

You say Agent does "Open an issue for the Safari login bug" create with bug label "Track bug: API returns 500 on /users" check duplicates, then create "Create GitHub task for dark mode" create with task label "List open issues" list "Comment on issue #15: fix in PR #99" comment 15 "Close issue #8, resolved" close 8 "Import issues from manifest" import --dry-run then --apply "Apply labels on the repo" labels --apply

Italian works too:

Tu dici L'agente fa «Apri issue per il bug del login su Safari» create con label bug «Elenca le issue aperte» list «Chiudi issue #8, risolto» close 8

docs.title: Instruction files
docs.text:
Platform File Universal AGENTS.md Cursor .cursor/rules/github-issues.mdc Claude Code CLAUDE.md Copilot .github/copilot-instructions.md

See Platforms for full setup.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Run all commands from the project root (where scripts/ lives). Same CLI on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

docs.title: Labels
docs.text:
Defined in tracker/labels.yml. First time on a repo:

python scripts/github_issue.py labels
python scripts/github_issue.py labels --apply

docs.title: List issues
docs.text:
python scripts/github_issue.py list
python scripts/github_issue.py list --state closed --limit 50

docs.title: Create issue
docs.text:
python scripts/github_issue.py create "Short title" --body "Markdown **description**" --labels bug,priority-high,area-frontend

From file:

python scripts/github_issue.py create "Safari bug" --body-file description.md --labels bug

docs.title: Comment
docs.text: python scripts/github_issue.py comment 42 --body "Fix applied in commit abc123"

docs.title: Close
docs.text:
python scripts/github_issue.py close 42
python scripts/github_issue.py close 42 --reason "Duplicate of #40"
python scripts/github_issue.py close-batch 10 11 12 --reason "Deprioritized backlog"

docs.title: Import batch (migrate from local tracker)
docs.text:
cp tracker/import-manifest.example.json tracker/import-manifest.json
# edit import-manifest.json
python scripts/github_issue.py import --dry-run
python scripts/github_issue.py import --apply

Include Legacy ID: in each imported body to prevent duplicates on re-import:

**Legacy ID:** `BUG-001`

docs.title: Override repo slug
docs.text: python scripts/github_issue.py --repo myorg/my-app list

docs.title: Labels
docs.text:
Conventions in tracker/labels.yml. Customize and re-run labels --apply.

Label When to use bug Wrong behavior, regression enhancement New feature, UX improvement task Planned work with definition of done documentation Docs only priority-high Blocks release or critical flow priority-medium Important but not blocking priority-low Nice-to-have, backlog area-frontend UI, client, browser area-backend API, services, database area-infra CI, Docker, deploy, monitoring area-docs README, guides, site imported Migrated from previous tracker

For imported issues, include Legacy ID: in the body (e.g. BUG-001) and a link to the local markdown file.

Apply to GitHub:

python scripts/github_issue.py labels --apply

docs.title: Adopt into a project
docs.text:
From the issuebeam folder (or after cloning this repo):

python scripts/adopt.py --target ../my-repo --repo myorg/my-app

Use --force to overwrite existing files. Works on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

docs.title: What adopt.py copies
docs.text: Item Purpose scripts/github_issue.py CLI tracker/* Labels, examples, slug file .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/* Web forms .github/copilot-instructions.md GitHub Copilot .cursor/rules/github-issues.mdc Cursor AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md Universal / Claude Code rules

docs.title: What it creates
docs.text:
• tracker/github_repo with your slug
  • tracker/import-manifest.json from example (if missing)
  • .secrets/.gitkeep

docs.title: After adopt
docs.text:
cd ../my-repo
python scripts/github_issue.py labels --apply
python scripts/github_issue.py list

Open the folder in your AI agent:

Platform Instruction file Cursor .cursor/rules/github-issues.mdc Claude Code CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md Copilot .github/copilot-instructions.md + AGENTS.md Other See Platforms

Full documentation: issuebeam.github.io/docs

docs.title: The problem
docs.text:
With LLMs and fast iteration:

  • Bugs discussed in chat are forgotten
  • Tasks stay "mental" but never appear in the team backlog
  • Scattered markdown files (TODO.md, bugs.txt) multiply
  • Nobody knows what is open, in progress, or closed

docs.title: The solution
docs.text:
Issuebeam connects AI agent chat to GitHub Issues with:

  1. scripts/github_issue.py — stdlib Python CLI
  2. Agent instructions — files the LLM reads (AGENTS.md, Cursor rule, Copilot, Claude, …)
  3. GitHub templates — web forms for browser-based issue creation
  4. adopt.py — copy the skeleton into any repo in one command

GitHub Issues becomes the source of truth. Local markdown stays for detailed plans and archives — not operational status.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
You in chat  →  AI agent (reads AGENTS.md)  →  github_issue.py  →  GitHub Issues

Requirements for any platform:

  • Python 3 (Windows, macOS, Linux)
  • GITHUB_TOKEN with Issues read/write
  • Repo slug in tracker/github_repo or GITHUB_REPO
  • Agent instructions: run the script, don't delegate to the user

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
1. GitHub token
  2. Repository slug
  3. Adopt into a project (optional)
  4. Choose your platform

docs.title: Repository slug
docs.text:
The CLI must know which GitHub repo to use (owner/name).

Resolution order:

Priority Source 1 GITHUB_REPO environment variable 2 .env → GITHUB_REPO=owner/repo 3 tracker/github_repo — one line (created by adopt.py)

docs.title: Option A — local file (recommended after adopt)
docs.text:
Cross-platform (Python 3):

python -c "from pathlib import Path; Path('tracker/github_repo').write_text('myorg/my-app\n')"

docs.title: Option B — .env
docs.text:
GITHUB_REPO=myorg/my-app
GITHUB_TOKEN=github_pat_...

docs.title: Option C — one-time override
docs.text: python scripts/github_issue.py --repo myorg/my-app list

docs.title: First commands
docs.text:
After token and slug are set:

python scripts/github_issue.py labels --apply
python scripts/github_issue.py list

Works the same on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

docs.title: GitHub token
docs.text:
You need a Personal Access Token with permission to read and write issues on the target repository.

Issuebeam runs on Windows, macOS, and Linux — only the way you export GITHUB_TOKEN changes. The CLI is the same everywhere: python scripts/github_issue.py.

docs.title: Classic token
docs.text:
1. GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Tokens (classic)
  2. Generate new token (classic)
  3. Minimum scope: repo (or Issues-only with fine-grained on a single repo)
  4. Copy the token — shown once

docs.title: Fine-grained token (recommended for teams)
docs.text:
1. Personal access tokens → Fine-grained tokens → Generate new token
  2. Repository access: only the project repo (e.g. myorg/my-app)
  3. Permissions → Issues: Read and write
  4. No other permissions required

docs.title: Where to store the token
docs.text:
Method Works on Pros Cons GITHUB_TOKEN env var Win · Mac · Linux Works in terminals and AI agents Must restart IDE/shell after change .secrets/github_token All Gitignored, simple, portable Local file — never commit .env All Convenient in dev Risk of accidental commit (gitignored)

Never paste the token in agent chat, public issues, or commits.

docs.title: Set GITHUB_TOKEN (recommended)
docs.text:
Windows
  1. Open Environment Variables → User variables → New…
  2. Name: GITHUB_TOKEN — Value: github_pat_...
  3. OK on all dialogs
  4. Restart your IDE (Cursor, VS Code, …) or open a new terminal

Extra: if the IDE terminal still misses the variable, issuebeam also reads the Windows user registry (same value as above) — no extra setup.

macOS

Terminal / CLI agents — add to ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc:

export GITHUB_TOKEN=github_pat_...

Then source ~/.zshrc (or open a new terminal).

GUI apps (Cursor, VS Code launched from Dock) — user env vars from the shell are not always inherited. Options:

  • Launch the IDE from a terminal: cursor . or code .
  • Or use .env / .secrets/github_token in the project (see below)
Linux

Shell — add to ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, or use direnv in the repo:

export GITHUB_TOKEN=github_pat_...

systemd user session (optional, for GUI tools):

# ~/.config/environment.d/github.conf
GITHUB_TOKEN=github_pat_...

Log out and back in. Alternatively use .env or .secrets/github_token.

docs.title: Alternative: .env or .secrets/ (all OS)
docs.text:
.env at repo root (gitignored):

GITHUB_REPO=myorg/my-app
GITHUB_TOKEN=github_pat_...

.secrets/github_token — one line, gitignored:

mkdir -p .secrets
printf '%s\n' 'github_pat_...' > .secrets/github_token
chmod 600 .secrets/github_token   # macOS / Linux

docs.title: Verify
docs.text:
python -c "import os; print('OK' if os.environ.get('GITHUB_TOKEN') else 'MISSING')"

Then (after repository slug is set):

python scripts/github_issue.py list

docs.title: Token resolution order (CLI)
docs.text:
The script reads the token automatically:

  1. GITHUB_TOKEN in the current process environment
  2. Windows only: user env var from registry (helps IDE terminals)
  3. .env in repo root
  4. .secrets/github_token

docs.title: Optional: GITHUB_REPO in the environment
docs.text:
Same methods as GITHUB_TOKEN — useful if you always work on one repo:

export GITHUB_REPO=myorg/my-app

docs.title: Claude Code
docs.text: Claude Code (VS Code extension, CLI, or desktop) reads CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md from the repository root.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Run adopt — copies CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md.
  2. Open the repository root in VS Code (not a parent folder).
  3. Install Claude Code extension or use the CLI.
  4. Set GITHUB_TOKEN (token guide).

docs.title: Verify
docs.text: Ask: "Create a GitHub issue titled [Bug] Test from Claude" — Claude should execute the Python script.

docs.title: VS Code tips
docs.text:
• Shell commands run from integrated terminal inherit user env vars after IDE restart.
  • If Claude asks you to run commands manually, remind it to follow AGENTS.md.

docs.title: Optional project memory
docs.text:
Add to Claude project settings:

For bugs and tasks, use python scripts/github_issue.py per AGENTS.md. GitHub Issues is the source of truth.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Problem Fix Wrong working directory Open repo root where scripts/ lives Agent doesn't run script Ensure AGENTS.md is in context Token missing Restart VS Code after env var change

docs.title: GitHub Copilot
docs.text:
Copilot uses workspace instructions:

.github/copilot-instructions.md
AGENTS.md

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Adopt issuebeam — both files are included.
  2. Enable Copilot Chat with agent / edit and terminal capabilities in VS Code.
  3. Configure token and repository slug.

docs.title: Visual Studio
docs.text: Same copilot-instructions.md pattern when Copilot workspace context is enabled. The solution folder must be the repo root where scripts/ lives.

docs.title: Verify
docs.text: In Copilot Chat: "Track this bug on GitHub using our issuebeam CLI".

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Problem Fix Works in Cursor, not Copilot Check .github/copilot-instructions.md exists Agent won't run commands Enable terminal permissions in Copilot settings Wrong repo Open solution at repository root

docs.title: Cursor
docs.text:
Best-tested path. Issuebeam ships with:

.cursor/rules/github-issues.mdc   # alwaysApply: true

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Clone issuebeam or run adopt.py into your repo.
  2. Configure token and repository slug.
  3. Open the folder in Cursor — the rule is active immediately.

docs.title: Verify
docs.text: In chat: "List open GitHub issues" → agent should run python scripts/github_issue.py list.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Works in Agent mode and integrated terminal on all OS.
  • On Windows, GITHUB_TOKEN from user env can also be read via registry if the IDE shell does not inherit it.
  • Italian phrases like «apri issue per…» are documented in the rule file.
  • This rule is Cursor-specific; other platforms use AGENTS.md — see overview.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Problem Fix Agent asks you to paste commands Point to AGENTS.md: execute script directly Rule not applied Check alwaysApply: true in .mdc front matter Token not found Restart Cursor after setting GITHUB_TOKEN

docs.title: Other agents
docs.text: Copy AGENTS.md into the format your tool expects. The CLI is identical on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

docs.title: Platform reference
docs.text: Platform Instruction file Setup Windsurf .windsurfrules cp AGENTS.md .windsurfrules Cline .clinerules or custom instructions Paste AGENTS.md in Cline settings Continue.dev .continue/rules or YAML config Reference AGENTS.md in config Gemini CLI GEMINI.md cp AGENTS.md GEMINI.md OpenAI Codex CLI AGENTS.md Included by adopt — run from repo root Aider CONVENTIONS.md or /read AGENTS.md Load at session start Custom / CI AGENTS.md in system prompt Grant scoped shell tool

docs.title: Manual use (no AI)
docs.text:
No agent required:

python scripts/github_issue.py create "Bug: login redirect" --labels bug
python scripts/github_issue.py list

Many teams adopt issuebeam for CLI + labels + templates first, then add agent rules per IDE.

docs.title: Custom agents and CI
docs.text:
Any automation that runs Python can use issuebeam without an LLM:

python scripts/github_issue.py create "CI: flaky test on main" --labels bug,area-infra

For custom LLM agents:

  • Put AGENTS.md in system prompt or RAG context
  • Grant a shell tool scoped to the repo
  • Never pass the token through the model — use env vars only

docs.title: Supported platforms
docs.text: Issuebeam is not tied to Cursor. If your agent can read repo files and run shell commands, it works.

docs.title: Quick comparison
docs.text: Platform Instruction file(s) Shell access Cursor .cursor/rules/github-issues.mdc Yes Claude Code CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md Yes GitHub Copilot .github/copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md Yes Other agents AGENTS.md or platform equivalent Yes Manual — You run CLI

docs.title: Same CLI everywhere
docs.text: Layer Platform-specific? scripts/github_issue.py No Token, slug, labels No Agent instruction file Yes — path differs

docs.title: Mixed teams
docs.text:
You can ship all instruction files in one repo — they do not conflict:

  • Cursor reads .cursor/rules/
  • Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md
  • Copilot reads copilot-instructions.md
  • All point to the same CLI and conventions

docs.title: Choosing files
docs.text:
You use… Minimum files Cursor only .cursor/rules/github-issues.mdc Claude Code only AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md Copilot only AGENTS.md + .github/copilot-instructions.md Mixed team All of the above

adopt.py copies the common set automatically.

docs.title: What is Issuebeam?
docs.text: A lightweight skeleton that connects AI coding agents to GitHub Issues. The agent runs a Python CLI to create, comment, and close issues — no gh CLI, no PowerShell scripts.

docs.title: Is it Cursor-only?
docs.text: No. Cursor is the best-tested path, but the same CLI works with Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, Cline, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, Aider, and manual use. See Platforms.

docs.title: Do I need the gh CLI?
docs.text: No. Issuebeam uses stdlib Python (urllib) against the GitHub REST API. You need Python and a PAT with Issues read/write.

docs.title: How does the agent know to use it?
docs.text: Instruction files in the repo: AGENTS.md (universal), .cursor/rules/ for Cursor, CLAUDE.md for Claude Code, .github/copilot-instructions.md for Copilot. The agent runs python scripts/github_issue.py directly.

docs.title: Can I adopt it into an existing project?
docs.text: Yes: python scripts/adopt.py --target ../my-repo --repo myorg/my-app

docs.title: Where does the token live?
docs.text: Prefer GITHUB_TOKEN as an environment variable (works on Windows, macOS, Linux). Alternatives: .env (gitignored) or .secrets/github_token. On Windows, the CLI can also read user env vars from the registry if the IDE terminal does not inherit them.

docs.title: Can I use it without any AI agent?
docs.text: Yes. The CLI is fully usable by humans — many teams start with CLI + labels, then add agent rules.

docs.title: How do I publish these docs?
docs.text:
pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
python scripts/publish_docs.py
cd ../issuebeam.github.io
git push

Site: issuebeam.github.io/docs

docs.title: Origin
docs.text: Issuebeam is a generalized extract of the tracking system used in the Qwibo project (audio transcription + LLM summarization).

docs.title: Removed from Qwibo context
docs.text:
• Product-specific labels and templates (ASR, Electron, Docker)
  • Path data/.secrets/ → replaced with .secrets/
  • Variable QWIBO_GITHUB_REPO → GITHUB_REPO + tracker/github_repo

docs.title: Kept
docs.text:
• Stdlib CLI without gh dependency — Windows, macOS, Linux
  • Token from env, .env, .secrets/, and on Windows optional registry read for IDE agents
  • Multi-platform instructions (AGENTS.md + Cursor/Copilot/Claude rules)
  • Import manifest with Legacy ID anti-duplicates
  • Agent rule: run the script, don't delegate to the user

docs.title: Name
docs.text:
Issuebeam evokes a beam connecting chat to the tracker — a direct bridge between conversation and the official backlog.

MIT — Copyright © 2026 Antonio Trento.

docs.title: Do
docs.text:
• Store token in user env vars, .env (gitignored), or .secrets/github_token
  • Ensure .gitignore includes .secrets/ and .env
  • Use fine-grained tokens scoped to a single repository
  • Rotate the token if accidentally exposed

docs.title: Don't
docs.text:
• Never paste the token in agent chat or GitHub issues
  • Never commit tokens or share screenshots with tokens visible
  • Never use PowerShell scripts in this stack (antivirus / team policy) — Python only

docs.title: SSL / corporate proxy
docs.text:
If you see SSL certificate errors toward api.github.com (common on Windows with corporate AV/proxy, sometimes elsewhere):

pip install -r requirements-optional.txt

Installs optional truststore (uses the OS trust store on Windows).

docs.title: Token resolution order (CLI)
docs.text:
The CLI reads the token automatically:

  1. GITHUB_TOKEN in process environment (all OS)
  2. Windows only: user env var from registry (helps IDE terminals)
  3. .env at repo root
  4. .secrets/github_token

The agent should run the script directly — not ask the user to paste tokens or commands when configured.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Symptom Solution ERRORE: token GitHub non trovato / token not found Set GITHUB_TOKEN or create .secrets/github_token ERRORE: repository GitHub non configurato / repo not configured Create tracker/github_repo or GITHUB_REPO in .env HTTP 401 Token expired or revoked — generate a new one HTTP 404 Wrong repo slug or token has no access HTTP 403 Insufficient permissions — Issues read/write required SSL certificate error pip install -r requirements-optional.txt (truststore) Agent never runs the script Add instruction file for your platform — see Platforms Agent asks user to paste commands Strengthen AGENTS.md: execute script directly Works in Cursor, not Copilot Check .github/copilot-instructions.md and AGENTS.md Works in terminal, not in agent Enable shell/terminal permissions in agent settings Wrong working directory Open repository root where scripts/ lives

docs.title: Verify setup
docs.text:
python -c "import os; print('token:', 'OK' if os.environ.get('GITHUB_TOKEN') else 'MISSING')"
cat tracker/github_repo
python scripts/github_issue.py list

docs.title: Get help
docs.text: Open an issue on github.com/issuebeam/issuebeam.

docs.title: Issuebeam
docs.text:
← Sito web (issuebeam.github.io) · Documentazione

Issuebeam collega agenti AI di coding a GitHub Issues con una CLI Python stdlib e file di istruzioni nel repository. Funziona su Windows, macOS e Linux con Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot e altro.

Documentazione bilingue

Questa documentazione è disponibile anche in inglese. Usa il selettore lingua in alto.

docs.title: Cosa fa
docs.text: Livello Descrizione CLI scripts/github_issue.py — crea, elenca, commenta, chiude, importa issue Regole agente AGENTS.md, regola Cursor, istruzioni Copilot, CLAUDE.md, … GitHub Backlog ufficiale con label, template web, Fixes #N nelle PR

docs.title: A chi serve
docs.text:
Sviluppi con vibe coding (LLM, iterazione rapida) e perdi bug e task sepolti in chat. Issuebeam sposta lo stato operativo su GitHub Issues — non su markdown sparsi.

Non solo Cursor. La stessa CLI funziona ovunque; cambia solo il percorso del file istruzioni per IDE.

docs.title: Avvio rapido
docs.text:
python -c "from pathlib import Path; Path('tracker/github_repo').write_text('myorg/my-app\n')"
python scripts/github_issue.py labels --apply
python scripts/github_issue.py create "Test setup" --labels task
python scripts/github_issue.py list

Dettagli: Panoramica · Token · Piattaforme

docs.title: Adotta nel tuo repo
docs.text:
python scripts/adopt.py --target ../my-repo --repo myorg/my-app

Copia CLI, label, template e file istruzioni per più piattaforme.

docs.title: Mappa documentazione
docs.text: Sezione Contenuto Per iniziare Problema, architettura, setup Piattaforme Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, altro Riferimento CLI Tutti i comandi Flusso agente Frasi in linguaggio naturale Riferimento FAQ, sicurezza, troubleshooting

docs.title: Anteprima e pubblicazione docs
docs.text:
pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
mkdocs serve

Anteprima su http://127.0.0.1:8000 — pubblicazione: python scripts/publish_docs.py, poi git push nel repo gemello issuebeam.github.io. Nessuna GitHub Action.

docs.title: Licenza
docs.text: MIT — Copyright © 2026 Antonio Trento. Vedi LICENSE.

docs.title: Commit e PR
docs.text:
Quando una PR risolve lavoro tracciato, referenzia l'issue nel messaggio di commit:

fix(auth): handle Safari redirect after login

Fixes #42

GitHub chiude automaticamente l'issue al merge (se l'opzione del repo è attiva).

Varianti:

  • Closes #42 — stesso comportamento di chiusura automatica
  • Refs #42 — riferimento senza chiusura automatica

docs.title: Flusso agente
docs.text:
Dopo aver corretto un bug, l'agente dovrebbe:

  1. Committare con Fixes #N quando appropriato
  2. Opzionalmente commentare l'issue: python scripts/github_issue.py comment N --body "Fix in …"
  3. Chiudere manualmente se serve: python scripts/github_issue.py close N

docs.title: Linguaggio naturale
docs.text:
Con le istruzioni agente attive (AGENTS.md, regola Cursor, Copilot, …) descrivi il lavoro in linguaggio naturale — non serve ricordare la sintassi CLI.

Tu dici L'agente fa «Apri issue per il bug del login su Safari» create con label bug «Traccia bug: API restituisce 500 su /users» verifica duplicati, poi create «Crea task GitHub per dark mode» create con label task «Elenca le issue aperte» list «Commenta issue #15: fix in PR #99» comment 15 «Chiudi issue #8, risolto» close 8 «Importa le issue dal manifest» import --dry-run poi --apply «Applica le label sul repo» labels --apply

Funziona anche in inglese:

You say Agent does "Open an issue for the Safari login bug" create with bug label "List open issues" list

docs.title: File istruzioni
docs.text:
Piattaforma File Universale AGENTS.md Cursor .cursor/rules/github-issues.mdc Claude Code CLAUDE.md Copilot .github/copilot-instructions.md

Vedi Piattaforme per il setup completo.

docs.title: Comandi
docs.text: Esegui tutti i comandi dalla root del progetto (dove c'è scripts/). Stessa CLI su Windows, macOS e Linux.

docs.title: Label
docs.text:
Definite in tracker/labels.yml. Prima volta sul repo:

python scripts/github_issue.py labels
python scripts/github_issue.py labels --apply

docs.title: Elenco issue
docs.text:
python scripts/github_issue.py list
python scripts/github_issue.py list --state closed --limit 50

docs.title: Creare issue
docs.text:
python scripts/github_issue.py create "Titolo breve" --body "Descrizione in **markdown**" --labels bug,priority-high,area-frontend

Da file:

python scripts/github_issue.py create "Bug Safari" --body-file descrizione.md --labels bug

docs.title: Commento
docs.text: python scripts/github_issue.py comment 42 --body "Fix applicato in commit abc123"

docs.title: Chiusura
docs.text:
python scripts/github_issue.py close 42
python scripts/github_issue.py close 42 --reason "Duplicato di #40"
python scripts/github_issue.py close-batch 10 11 12 --reason "Backlog non prioritario"

docs.title: Import batch (migrazione da tracker locale)
docs.text:
cp tracker/import-manifest.example.json tracker/import-manifest.json
# modifica import-manifest.json
python scripts/github_issue.py import --dry-run
python scripts/github_issue.py import --apply

Includi Legacy ID: nel body di ogni voce importata per evitare duplicati:

**Legacy ID:** `BUG-001`

docs.title: Override slug repo
docs.text: python scripts/github_issue.py --repo myorg/my-app list

docs.title: Label
docs.text:
Convenzioni in tracker/labels.yml. Personalizza e riesegui labels --apply.

Label Quando usarla bug Comportamento errato, regressione enhancement Nuova funzionalità, miglioramento UX task Lavoro pianificato con DoD documentation Solo documentazione priority-high Blocca release o flusso critico priority-medium Importante ma non bloccante priority-low Nice-to-have, backlog area-frontend UI, client, browser area-backend API, servizi, database area-infra CI, Docker, deploy, monitoring area-docs README, guide, sito imported Migrato da tracker precedente

Per issue importate, includi Legacy ID: nel body (es. BUG-001) e link al file markdown locale.

Applica su GitHub:

python scripts/github_issue.py labels --apply

docs.title: Adotta in un progetto
docs.text:
Dalla cartella issuebeam (o dopo aver clonato questo repo):

python scripts/adopt.py --target ../my-repo --repo myorg/my-app

Usa --force per sovrascrivere file esistenti. Funziona su Windows, macOS e Linux.

docs.title: Cosa copia adopt.py
docs.text: Elemento Scopo scripts/github_issue.py CLI tracker/* Label, esempi, file slug .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/* Form web .github/copilot-instructions.md GitHub Copilot .cursor/rules/github-issues.mdc Cursor AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md Regole universali / Claude Code

docs.title: Cosa crea
docs.text:
• tracker/github_repo con il tuo slug
  • tracker/import-manifest.json dall'esempio (se mancante)
  • .secrets/.gitkeep

docs.title: Dopo l'adopt
docs.text:
cd ../my-repo
python scripts/github_issue.py labels --apply
python scripts/github_issue.py list

Apri la cartella nel tuo agente AI:

Piattaforma File istruzioni Cursor .cursor/rules/github-issues.mdc Claude Code CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md Copilot .github/copilot-instructions.md + AGENTS.md Altro Vedi Piattaforme

Documentazione completa: issuebeam.github.io/docs/it

docs.title: Il problema
docs.text:
Con LLM e iterazione rapida succede spesso:

  • Un bug discusso in chat viene dimenticato
  • Un task resta «mentale» ma non compare nel backlog del team
  • File markdown sparsi (TODO.md, bugs.txt) moltiplicano la confusione
  • Nessuno sa cosa è aperto, in corso o chiuso

docs.title: La soluzione
docs.text:
Issuebeam collega la chat dell'agente AI a GitHub Issues con:

  1. scripts/github_issue.py — CLI Python stdlib
  2. Istruzioni agente — file letti dall'LLM (AGENTS.md, regola Cursor, Copilot, Claude, …)
  3. Template GitHub — form web per aprire issue dal browser
  4. adopt.py — copia lo skeleton in qualsiasi repo con un comando

GitHub Issues diventa la fonte di verità. I markdown locali restano per piani dettagliati e archivio — non per lo stato operativo.

docs.title: Architettura
docs.text:
Tu in chat  →  Agente AI (legge AGENTS.md)  →  github_issue.py  →  GitHub Issues

Requisiti per ogni piattaforma:

  • Python 3 (Windows, macOS, Linux)
  • GITHUB_TOKEN con Issues read/write
  • Slug in tracker/github_repo o GITHUB_REPO
  • Istruzioni agente: esegui lo script, non delegare all'utente

docs.title: Prossimi passi
docs.text:
1. Token GitHub
  2. Slug repository
  3. Adotta in un progetto (opzionale)
  4. Scegli la piattaforma

docs.title: Slug repository
docs.text:
La CLI deve sapere quale repo GitHub usare (owner/nome).

Ordine di risoluzione:

Priorità Sorgente 1 Variabile d'ambiente GITHUB_REPO 2 .env → GITHUB_REPO=owner/repo 3 tracker/github_repo — una riga (creato da adopt.py)

docs.title: Opzione A — file locale (consigliata dopo adopt)
docs.text:
Cross-platform (Python 3):

python -c "from pathlib import Path; Path('tracker/github_repo').write_text('myorg/my-app\n')"

docs.title: Opzione B — .env
docs.text:
GITHUB_REPO=myorg/my-app
GITHUB_TOKEN=github_pat_...

docs.title: Opzione C — override una tantum
docs.text: python scripts/github_issue.py --repo myorg/my-app list

docs.title: Primi comandi
docs.text:
Dopo token e slug:

python scripts/github_issue.py labels --apply
python scripts/github_issue.py list

Uguale su Windows, macOS e Linux.

docs.title: Token GitHub
docs.text:
Serve un Personal Access Token con permesso di leggere e scrivere le issue del repository target.

Issuebeam funziona su Windows, macOS e Linux — cambia solo il modo in cui esporti GITHUB_TOKEN. La CLI è identica ovunque: python scripts/github_issue.py.

docs.title: Classic token
docs.text:
1. GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Tokens (classic)
  2. Generate new token (classic)
  3. Scope minimo: repo (oppure solo Issues con fine-grained sul singolo repo)
  4. Copia il token — lo vedi una sola volta

docs.title: Fine-grained token (consigliato per team)
docs.text:
1. Personal access tokens → Fine-grained tokens → Generate new token
  2. Repository access: solo il repo del progetto (es. myorg/my-app)
  3. Permissions → Issues: Read and write
  4. Nessun altro permesso necessario

docs.title: Dove mettere il token
docs.text:
Metodo Funziona su Pro Contro Variabile GITHUB_TOKEN Win · Mac · Linux Terminale e agenti AI Riavvia IDE/shell dopo la modifica .secrets/github_token Tutti Gitignored, semplice, portabile File locale — mai committare .env Tutti Comodo in dev Rischio commit accidentale (gitignored)

Mai incollare il token in chat con l'agente, issue pubbliche o commit.

docs.title: Imposta GITHUB_TOKEN (consigliato)
docs.text:
Windows
  1. Apri Variabili d'ambiente → Variabili utente → Nuova…
  2. Nome: GITHUB_TOKEN — Valore: github_pat_...
  3. OK su tutte le finestre
  4. Riavvia l'IDE (Cursor, VS Code, …) o apri un nuovo terminale

Extra: se il terminale dell'IDE non vede ancora la variabile, issuebeam legge anche il registry utente Windows (stesso valore) — nessun setup aggiuntivo.

macOS

Terminale / agenti CLI — aggiungi a ~/.zshrc o ~/.bashrc:

export GITHUB_TOKEN=github_pat_...

Poi source ~/.zshrc (o nuovo terminale).

App GUI (Cursor, VS Code dal Dock) — le variabili della shell non sempre arrivano alle app avviate dal launcher. Opzioni:

  • Avvia l'IDE dal terminale: cursor . o code .
  • Oppure usa .env / .secrets/github_token nel progetto (sotto)
Linux

Shell — aggiungi a ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, o usa direnv nel repo:

export GITHUB_TOKEN=github_pat_...

Sessione utente systemd (opzionale, per tool GUI):

# ~/.config/environment.d/github.conf
GITHUB_TOKEN=github_pat_...

Logout e login. In alternativa .env o .secrets/github_token.

docs.title: Alternativa: .env o .secrets/ (tutti i SO)
docs.text:
.env nella root del repo (gitignored):

GITHUB_REPO=myorg/my-app
GITHUB_TOKEN=github_pat_...

.secrets/github_token — una riga, gitignored:

mkdir -p .secrets
printf '%s\n' 'github_pat_...' > .secrets/github_token
chmod 600 .secrets/github_token   # macOS / Linux

docs.title: Verifica
docs.text:
python -c "import os; print('OK' if os.environ.get('GITHUB_TOKEN') else 'MANCANTE')"

Poi (dopo lo slug repository):

python scripts/github_issue.py list

docs.title: Ordine di lettura token (CLI)
docs.text:
Lo script legge il token automaticamente:

  1. GITHUB_TOKEN nell'ambiente del processo corrente
  2. Solo Windows: variabile utente da registry (aiuta i terminali IDE)
  3. .env nella root del repo
  4. .secrets/github_token

docs.title: Opzionale: GITHUB_REPO nell'ambiente
docs.text:
Stessi metodi di GITHUB_TOKEN — utile se lavori sempre sullo stesso repo:

export GITHUB_REPO=myorg/my-app

docs.title: Claude Code
docs.text: Claude Code (estensione VS Code, CLI o desktop) legge CLAUDE.md e AGENTS.md dalla root del repository.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Esegui adopt — copia CLAUDE.md e AGENTS.md.
  2. Apri la root del repository in VS Code (non una cartella padre).
  3. Installa l'estensione Claude Code o usa la CLI.
  4. Imposta GITHUB_TOKEN (guida token).

docs.title: Verifica
docs.text: Chiedi: «Crea un'issue GitHub [Bug] Test da Claude» — Claude deve eseguire lo script Python.

docs.title: Suggerimenti VS Code
docs.text:
• I comandi shell nel terminale integrato ereditano le variabili utente dopo il riavvio dell'IDE.
  • Se Claude chiede comandi manuali, rimanda a AGENTS.md.

docs.title: Memoria progetto (opzionale)
docs.text:
Aggiungi nelle impostazioni progetto Claude:

Per bug e task, usa python scripts/github_issue.py secondo AGENTS.md. GitHub Issues è la fonte di verità.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Problema Soluzione Directory sbagliata Apri la root dove c'è scripts/ L'agente non esegue lo script Verifica che AGENTS.md sia nel contesto Token mancante Riavvia VS Code dopo la variabile d'ambiente

docs.title: GitHub Copilot
docs.text:
Copilot usa le istruzioni workspace:

.github/copilot-instructions.md
AGENTS.md

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Adotta issuebeam — entrambi i file sono inclusi.
  2. Abilita Copilot Chat con capacità agent / edit e terminale in VS Code.
  3. Configura token e slug repository.

docs.title: Visual Studio
docs.text: Stesso schema copilot-instructions.md quando il contesto workspace Copilot è attivo. La cartella soluzione deve essere la root del repo dove c'è scripts/.

docs.title: Verifica
docs.text: In Copilot Chat: "Track this bug on GitHub using our issuebeam CLI".

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Problema Soluzione Funziona in Cursor, non in Copilot Controlla che esista .github/copilot-instructions.md L'agente non esegue comandi Abilita permessi terminale nelle impostazioni Copilot Repo sbagliato Apri la soluzione alla root del repository

docs.title: Cursor
docs.text:
Percorso più testato. Issuebeam include:

.cursor/rules/github-issues.mdc   # alwaysApply: true

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Clona issuebeam o esegui adopt.py nel tuo repo.
  2. Configura token e slug repository.
  3. Apri la cartella in Cursor — la regola è subito attiva.

docs.title: Verifica
docs.text: In chat: «Elenca le issue GitHub aperte» → l'agente deve eseguire python scripts/github_issue.py list.

docs.title: Note
docs.text:
• Funziona in modalità Agent e terminale integrato su tutti i SO.
  • Su Windows, GITHUB_TOKEN da variabile utente può essere letto anche via registry se la shell IDE non lo eredita.
  • Frasi italiane come «apri issue per…» sono documentate nella regola.
  • Questa regola è specifica per Cursor; altre piattaforme usano AGENTS.md — vedi panoramica.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Problema Soluzione L'agente chiede comandi da incollare Rimanda a AGENTS.md: eseguire lo script direttamente Regola non applicata Controlla alwaysApply: true nel front matter .mdc Token non trovato Riavvia Cursor dopo GITHUB_TOKEN

docs.title: Altri agenti
docs.text: Copia AGENTS.md nel formato richiesto dal tuo tool. La CLI è identica su Windows, macOS e Linux.

docs.title: Riferimento piattaforme
docs.text: Piattaforma File istruzioni Setup Windsurf .windsurfrules cp AGENTS.md .windsurfrules Cline .clinerules o istruzioni custom Incolla AGENTS.md nelle impostazioni Cline Continue.dev .continue/rules o config YAML Riferisci AGENTS.md nella config Gemini CLI GEMINI.md cp AGENTS.md GEMINI.md OpenAI Codex CLI AGENTS.md Incluso da adopt — esegui dalla root del repo Aider CONVENTIONS.md o /read AGENTS.md Carica all'avvio sessione Custom / CI AGENTS.md nel system prompt Concedi shell tool con scope sul repo

docs.title: Uso manuale (senza AI)
docs.text:
Nessun agente richiesto:

python scripts/github_issue.py create "Bug: login redirect" --labels bug
python scripts/github_issue.py list

Molti team adottano issuebeam per CLI + label + template prima, poi aggiungono regole agente per IDE.

docs.title: Agenti custom e CI
docs.text:
Qualsiasi automazione Python può usare issuebeam senza LLM:

python scripts/github_issue.py create "CI: flaky test on main" --labels bug,area-infra

Per agenti LLM custom:

  • Metti AGENTS.md nel system prompt o contesto RAG
  • Concedi uno shell tool con scope sul repo
  • Non passare mai il token al modello — solo variabili d'ambiente

docs.title: Piattaforme supportate
docs.text: Issuebeam non è legato a Cursor. Se il tuo agente legge file nel repo ed esegue comandi shell, funziona.

docs.title: Confronto rapido
docs.text: Piattaforma File istruzioni Accesso shell Cursor .cursor/rules/github-issues.mdc Sì Claude Code CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md Sì GitHub Copilot .github/copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md Sì Altri agenti AGENTS.md o equivalente Sì Manuale — Esegui tu la CLI

docs.title: Stessa CLI ovunque
docs.text: Livello Dipende dalla piattaforma? scripts/github_issue.py No Token, slug, label No File istruzioni agente Sì — cambia il percorso

docs.title: Team misti
docs.text:
Puoi includere tutti i file istruzioni in un repo — non vanno in conflitto:

  • Cursor legge .cursor/rules/
  • Claude Code legge CLAUDE.md
  • Copilot legge copilot-instructions.md
  • Tutti puntano alla stessa CLI e convenzioni

docs.title: Quale file usare
docs.text:
Usi… File minimi Solo Cursor .cursor/rules/github-issues.mdc Solo Claude Code AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md Solo Copilot AGENTS.md + .github/copilot-instructions.md Team misto Tutti i precedenti

adopt.py copia automaticamente il set comune.

docs.title: Cos'è Issuebeam?
docs.text: Uno skeleton leggero che collega agenti AI di coding a GitHub Issues. L'agente esegue una CLI Python per creare, commentare e chiudere issue — senza gh CLI e senza script PowerShell.

docs.title: Funziona solo con Cursor?
docs.text: No. Cursor è il percorso più testato, ma la stessa CLI funziona con Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, Cline, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, Aider e uso manuale. Vedi Piattaforme.

docs.title: Serve la gh CLI?
docs.text: No. Issuebeam usa Python stdlib (urllib) contro l'API REST di GitHub. Ti basta Python e un PAT con Issues read/write.

docs.title: Come fa l'agente a usarlo?
docs.text: File istruzioni nel repo: AGENTS.md (universale), .cursor/rules/ per Cursor, CLAUDE.md per Claude Code, .github/copilot-instructions.md per Copilot. L'agente esegue python scripts/github_issue.py direttamente.

docs.title: Posso adottarlo in un progetto esistente?
docs.text: Sì: python scripts/adopt.py --target ../my-repo --repo myorg/my-app

docs.title: Dove metto il token?
docs.text: Preferisci GITHUB_TOKEN come variabile d'ambiente (Windows, macOS, Linux). Alternative: .env (gitignored) o .secrets/github_token. Su Windows la CLI può leggere anche le variabili utente dal registry se il terminale IDE non le eredita.

docs.title: Posso usarlo senza agente AI?
docs.text: Sì. La CLI funziona anche per umani — molti team partono da CLI + label, poi aggiungono le regole agente.

docs.title: Come pubblico questa documentazione?
docs.text:
pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
python scripts/publish_docs.py
cd ../issuebeam.github.io
git push

Sito: issuebeam.github.io/docs/it

docs.title: Origine
docs.text: Issuebeam è un estratto generalizzato del sistema di tracking usato nel progetto Qwibo (trascrizione audio + riassunto LLM).

docs.title: Rimosso dal contesto Qwibo
docs.text:
• Label e template specifici del prodotto (ASR, Electron, Docker)
  • Path data/.secrets/ → sostituito con .secrets/
  • Variabile QWIBO_GITHUB_REPO → GITHUB_REPO + tracker/github_repo

docs.title: Mantenuto
docs.text:
• CLI stdlib senza dipendenza da gh — Windows, macOS, Linux
  • Token da env, .env, .secrets/, e su Windows lettura opzionale da registry per agenti IDE
  • Istruzioni multi-piattaforma (AGENTS.md + regole Cursor/Copilot/Claude)
  • Import manifest con Legacy ID anti-duplicati
  • Regola agente: esegui lo script, non delegare all'utente

docs.title: Nome
docs.text:
Issuebeam evoca un raggio che collega la chat al tracker — un ponte diretto tra conversazione e backlog ufficiale.

MIT — Copyright © 2026 Antonio Trento.

docs.title: Da fare
docs.text:
• Token solo in variabili utente, .env (gitignored) o .secrets/github_token
  • .gitignore include .secrets/ e .env
  • Token fine-grained limitato al singolo repository
  • Ruotare il token se esposto accidentalmente

docs.title: Da non fare
docs.text:
• Mai incollare il token in chat con l'agente o in issue GitHub
  • Mai committare token o condividere screenshot con token visibile
  • Mai usare script PowerShell in questo stack (antivirus / policy team) — solo Python

docs.title: SSL / proxy aziendale
docs.text:
Se vedi errori certificato SSL verso api.github.com (comune su Windows con AV/proxy aziendale, a volte anche altrove):

pip install -r requirements-optional.txt

Installa truststore opzionale (usa il trust store del SO su Windows).

docs.title: Ordine risoluzione token
docs.text:
La CLI legge il token automaticamente:

  1. GITHUB_TOKEN nell'ambiente del processo (tutti i SO)
  2. Solo Windows: variabile utente da registry (aiuta i terminali IDE)
  3. .env nella root del repo
  4. .secrets/github_token

L'agente deve eseguire lo script direttamente — non chiedere token o comandi all'utente se configurato.

docs.title: Risoluzione problemi
docs.text: Sintomo Soluzione ERRORE: token GitHub non trovato Imposta GITHUB_TOKEN o crea .secrets/github_token ERRORE: repository GitHub non configurato Crea tracker/github_repo o GITHUB_REPO in .env HTTP 401 Token scaduto o revocato — generane uno nuovo HTTP 404 Slug repo errato o token senza accesso HTTP 403 Permessi insufficienti — Issues read/write Errore certificato SSL pip install -r requirements-optional.txt (truststore) L'agente non esegue lo script Aggiungi file istruzioni per la piattaforma — vedi Piattaforme Chiede comandi da incollare Rafforza AGENTS.md: eseguire lo script direttamente Funziona in Cursor, non in Copilot Controlla .github/copilot-instructions.md e AGENTS.md Funziona in terminale, non in agente Abilita permessi shell/terminale nell'agente Directory sbagliata Apri la root del repo dove c'è scripts/

docs.title: Verifica setup
docs.text:
python -c "import os; print('token:', 'OK' if os.environ.get('GITHUB_TOKEN') else 'MANCANTE')"
cat tracker/github_repo
python scripts/github_issue.py list

docs.title: Aiuto
docs.text: Apri una issue su github.com/issuebeam/issuebeam.
